vllm.title: Refactor llama family models
vllm.author: esmeetu
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR is experimental and aiming for reducing redundant code in llama family models. We can make supporting those models in models/__init__.py's _MODELS variable like
"YiForCausalLM": ("llama", "LlamaForCausalLM")

and remove those repeated model files like llama model. Because some llama arch mod

vllm.title: Revert "Refactor llama family models"
vllm.author: pcmoritz
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Reverts #2637
Until we can fix the test failure https://buildkite.com/vllm/ci/builds/1165#018da191-b529-4516-af17-10858fd5b73e

vllm.title: Use CuPy for CUDA graphs
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This is a temporary fix for the memory leak issue when using CUDA graph w/o the custom all-reduce kernel. The PR uses CuPy NCCL instead of PyTorch NCCL.

vllm.title: Remove Yi model definition, please use `LlamaForCausalLM` instead
vllm.author: pcmoritz
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This is ported over from #2637 and removes the Yi model definition. The Yi architecture is the same as Llama and using LlamaForCausalLM instead has the advantage that it prevents code duplication and makes sure the Yi models inherit all the fixes we make for llama, like LoRA support.
This was the di

vllm.title: Add LoRA support for Mixtral
vllm.author: tterrysun
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Problem: We don't have LoRA support for Mixtral.
Solution: Add LoRA configurations for Mixtral and refactor relevant parts to allow this.
Testing: added correctness tests and updated existing tests.

vllm.title: Migrate InternLMForCausalLM to LlamaForCausalLM
vllm.author: pcmoritz
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The difference between InternLM and Llama is very small, just the bias for the attention layer.
For maintainability and to make things like LoRA support more uniform, this PR merges the two models. There should be no user-visible change.
This was proposed by @esmeetu in #2637 who is a coauthor of th

vllm.title: Fix internlm after https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/pull/2860
vllm.author: pcmoritz
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Sorry, I was shooting a little too fast with #2860 and it got merged before I could try it out end-to-end. This PR fixes the model, to verify the fix I ran the following:
In [1]: from vllm import LLM, SamplingParams

In [2]: prompts = [
   ...:     "Hello, my name is",
   ...:     "The president of

vllm.title: [Fix] Fix memory profiling when GPU is used by multiple processes
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Currently, the memory profiling logic assumes that the GPU memory is initially empty. However, this might not be always true since multiple processes can share a GPU. This PR fixes it by considering the other processes' GPU memory usage at the engine initialization time.
Original PR: #2249 by @hanzh
